mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses present value of future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A month-over-month increase in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points predicts negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE monthly change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline below 1100 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence. This contraction in housing demand predicts reduced aggregate demand and negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking markets price in demand weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1191.0 thousand units as of 2007-09-15
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1100, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO reflects real economic capacity utilization. Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO above 0.3% signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profitability and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales RSAFS and housing starts HOUST jointly measure consumer and residential demand. When both RSAFS declines month-over-month and HOUST falls below 1150 thousand units simultaneously, this signals synchronized demand contraction across consumption and investment, producing negative 20-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380259.0 million dollars as of 2007-10-15
fact:HOUST at 1191.0 thousand units as of 2007-09-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of dual-trigger instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (interaction coefficient p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS and unemployment rate UNRATE jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth slows below 50 thousand persons per month while UNRATE rises above 4.8%, this signals labor market inflection from expansion to contraction, predicting negative 60-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138421.0 thousand persons as of 2007-10-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of dual-trigger instances, or the joint signal shows no statistically significant incremental predictive power over univariate models (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy rate FEDFUNDS jointly determine real interest rates. When CPIAUCSL growth accelerates above 2.5% annually while FEDFUNDS remains below 5%, this signals monetary policy lag relative to inflation, creating financial instability that produces negative 20-day equity returns.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of dual-trigger instances, or the interaction term (inflation-rate gap) has coefficient p-value > 0.15 in predictive regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth GDPC1 is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. In late-cycle expansions when GDPC1 growth slows below 2% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 4.5%, equity valuations compress as growth-rate expectations decline and discount rates remain high, producing negative 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11630.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2007-07-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of dual-trigger instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
